steps.title: Rewrite the second equation to isolate the known ratio
steps.content:
The second equation $\frac{96j}{12xk} = 62$ can be rewritten by factoring out $\frac{j}{k}$:
$$\frac{96j}{12xk} = \frac{96}{12x} \cdot \frac{j}{k}$$
This separates the unknown variable $x$ from the ratio we know.

steps.title: Substitute the known value and simplify
steps.content:
Since we're told $\frac{j}{k} = 62$, substitute this into the rewritten equation:
$$\frac{96}{12x} \cdot 62 = 62$$
Divide both sides by 62:
$$\frac{96}{12x} = 1$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
From $\frac{96}{12x} = 1$, multiply both sides by $12x$:
$$96 = 12x$$
Divide by 12:
$$x = \frac{96}{12} = 8$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
